title: CAN differential signaling and bus states
description: CAN encodes dominant and recessive bits on a differential pair, and dominant bits win arbitration without data loss.
body: Use when reasoning about CAN arbitration and error behavior. Do not treat CAN like a single-ended bus, because its dominant-recessive scheme is what enables lossless arbitration. Actions: inspect the differential voltage for dominant versus recessive states, verify termination resistors at both ends, and check the recessive idle level. Validation signals include correct differential levels on a scope and arbitration resolving to the lowest identifier without retransmission. Risk boundary: missing termination causes reflections and intermittent errors, so flag any bus lacking proper end termination.
